reasoning.A:
DECISION: Volunteer: Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D  
REASONING: My puzzle (40 ÷ 8) is trivial, so I’m certain my part is correct. In all previous games, every initial arithmetic capsule has been correct and we’ve never seen conflicting answers when there’s only one capsule per question. Verifying would just burn coins and still leave some risk of random death if nobody volunteers. Given the strong track record and the simple nature of the puzzles, volunteering now with Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D is the best way to escape quickly while keeping all 4 coins.
reasoning.B:
DECISION: Volunteer: Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D  
REASONING: My puzzle (32 ÷ 4) is trivial, so I’m certain my own answer in Q2-B is correct. All previous games have been simple arithmetic with no wrong answers and there’s only one capsule per question, so the chance any of the four is wrong is extremely small. The bigger danger is a no-volunteer round causing a 1-in-4 random death. Verifying would just burn a coin while still leaving that risk, and passing relies on others stepping up. Volunteering now with the only available capsules maximizes my survival odds while keeping all 4 coins.
reasoning.C:
DECISION: Volunteer: Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D  
REASONING: My puzzle (42 ÷ 7) is trivial, so I’m fully confident my capsule Q3-C is correct. In all previous games, everyone’s answers to similarly simple arithmetic puzzles have been correct when revealed, so the chance that A, B, or D made an error is extremely small. Verifying would cost 1 coin and I still couldn’t guarantee all answers this round, while also risking a “no-volunteer” situation that could trigger a random death lottery like in Game 1. Volunteering now with the only available capsules (Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D) gives a very high chance of immediate escape while keeping all 4 of my coins.
